description: Complete redesign of the permission system. Replace the current 3-tier (observe/interact/sensitive) domain-based permission model and trust tier system with a simple 3-state (off/ask/auto) per-plugin/per-tool permission model. All tools are off by default. Remove TrustTier, PermissionsConfig, session permissions, confirmation countdown timer, domain matching, and the separate browserToolPolicy. Merge everything into a unified plugins config map. Replace the ConfirmationDialog countdown card with a proper retro Dialog modal. Add a skip-permissions banner to the side panel.

userStories.title: Define new ToolPermission type and PluginPermissionConfig in shared types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the shared type definitions updated so that all packages use the new 3-state permission model
acceptanceCriteria: ToolPermission type is 'off' | 'ask' | 'auto' (replaces the old 'allow' | 'ask' | 'deny')
PluginPermissionConfig interface has optional 'permission?: ToolPermission' and optional 'tools?: Record<string, ToolPermission>'
TrustTier type ('official' | 'community' | 'local') is removed from shared/src/index.ts
WirePluginPayload removes trustTier field, adds 'permission: ToolPermission' at plugin level, and each tool changes from 'enabled: boolean' to 'permission: ToolPermission'
ConfigStatePlugin removes trustTier field, adds 'permission: ToolPermission', and each tool changes from 'enabled: boolean' to 'permission: ToolPermission'
ConfigStateBrowserTool changes from 'enabled: boolean' to 'permission: ToolPermission'
New shared type ConfirmationRequest: { id: string; tool: string; plugin: string; params: Record<string, unknown> } — no domain, no tabId, no timeoutMs
New shared type ConfirmationResponse: { id: string; decision: 'allow' | 'deny'; alwaysAllow?: boolean } — no scope
OFFICIAL_SCOPE constant in shared/src/constants.ts is removed
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is the foundation story — all other stories depend on these types.

Files to modify:
- platform/shared/src/index.ts — Remove TrustTier type (line 92), update WirePluginPayload (line 200: remove trustTier, change tool.enabled to tool.permission), update ConfigStatePlugin (line 278: remove trustTier, change tools), update ConfigStateBrowserTool (line 304: enabled→permission). Add ToolPermission, PluginPermissionConfig, ConfirmationRequest, ConfirmationResponse types.
- platform/shared/src/constants.ts — Remove OFFICIAL_SCOPE constant (line 60).
- platform/shared/src/constants.test.ts — Remove OFFICIAL_SCOPE test.

IMPORTANT: Many downstream packages import these types. This story will cause type errors in mcp-server, browser-extension, CLI, and e2e. That is expected — later stories fix them. The acceptance criteria require only that the shared package itself builds and its own tests pass, plus the overall monorepo build. Since removing types will break consumers, you'll need to also add temporary 'TODO' stubs or update the type references in consumer packages just enough to make the build compile. Alternatively, export backward-compatible type aliases where needed (e.g., export type TrustTier = never) as deprecation stubs, but ONLY if removing them outright breaks the build. Prefer removing outright and fixing compile errors.

Actually, since this is a single-worker PRD with sequential stories, the build WILL break after this story if consumers still reference removed types. The safest approach: in this story, also grep for all imports of TrustTier and OFFICIAL_SCOPE across the entire monorepo and remove/update them. This is a large but mechanical change. Every file that imports TrustTier needs the import removed. Every file that uses TrustTier in a type annotation needs that annotation changed or removed. Search with: grep -rn 'TrustTier' platform/ e2e/ and grep -rn 'OFFICIAL_SCOPE' platform/ e2e/. The trustTier field appears in interfaces, function params, test fixtures, wire payloads, etc. Remove it everywhere.

For WirePluginPayload and ConfigStatePlugin: change `trustTier: TrustTier` → remove the field entirely. Change `enabled: boolean` → `permission: ToolPermission` on tool entries. Change the enabled field on ConfigStateBrowserTool similarly.

Test fixtures across ~30 files reference trustTier — remove those fields from all test fixtures.

userStories.title: Rewrite config.ts for the new plugin-centric permission config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want config.ts to read/write the new plugins permission structure so that tool permissions are stored per-plugin
acceptanceCriteria: OpentabsConfig type has 'plugins: Record<string, PluginPermissionConfig>' replacing 'tools', 'browserToolPolicy', and 'permissions' fields
OpentabsConfig retains 'localPlugins: string[]' and 'skipPermissions?: boolean'
PermissionsConfig type (trustedDomains, sensitiveDomains, toolPolicy, domainToolPolicy) is deleted entirely
Old ToolPermission type ('allow' | 'ask' | 'deny') in config.ts is removed — uses shared ToolPermission ('off' | 'ask' | 'auto')
parseConfigRecord() reads the new 'plugins' map and validates each entry as PluginPermissionConfig

parseConfigRecord() detects old-format 'tools: Record<string, boolean>' and/or 'browserToolPolicy' and migrates: boolean values map to 'off' (since new default is off, both true and false old values become irrelevant — discard them and let user re-configure)
savePluginPermissions() replaces both saveToolConfig() and saveBrowserToolPolicy() — single read-modify-write function persisting the 'plugins' field
defaultPermissions() is deleted
loadConfig() returns the new shape with empty 'plugins: {}' as default
config.test.ts is rewritten for the new config shape — tests config round-trip (save then load), migration from old format, parsing invalid values, default config
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts — This is the main file. Remove: ToolPermission type (line 26, the old allow/ask/deny one), PermissionsConfig interface (lines 29-38), VALID_TOOL_PERMISSIONS set (line 119), isToolPermission guard (line 121), defaultPermissions() (line 124), parsePermissionsConfig() (lines 132-173), browserToolPolicy parsing (lines 192-200), saveToolConfig() (lines 333-363), saveBrowserToolPolicy() (lines 375-403). Change OpentabsConfig (lines 40-51): remove 'tools: Record<string, boolean>', remove 'browserToolPolicy: Record<string, boolean>', remove 'permissions: PermissionsConfig', add 'plugins: Record<string, PluginPermissionConfig>'. Import ToolPermission and PluginPermissionConfig from @opentabs-dev/shared. Write new parsePluginsConfig() that validates each entry. Write new savePluginPermissions(state, plugins) that does the same read-modify-write pattern as the old functions.
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.test.ts — Rewrite tests.

Migration in parseConfigRecord(): detect old format by checking if record.tools exists and its values are booleans, or if record.browserToolPolicy exists. If so, log a migration notice and discard the old fields (return empty plugins: {}). The user will need to re-configure permissions in the new model. This is acceptable since the new default is 'off' for everything anyway — the user must explicitly enable tools.

The atomicWriteConfig, readConfigWithRetry, configWriteMutex, and configPath logic all stay the same — only the data shape changes.

userStories.title: Rewrite state.ts for the new permission model
userStories.description: As a developer, I want state.ts to use the new simplified permission state so that the server stores plugin permissions and simplified pending confirmations
acceptanceCriteria: ServerState replaces 'toolConfig: Record<string, boolean>' and 'browserToolPolicy: Record<string, boolean>' with 'pluginPermissions: Record<string, PluginPermissionConfig>'
ServerState removes 'permissions: PermissionsConfig' field
ServerState removes 'sessionPermissions: SessionPermissionRule[]' field
CONFIRMATION_TIMEOUT_MS constant is removed
SessionPermissionRule interface is removed
isSessionAllowed() function is removed
ConfirmationDecision type ('allow_once' | 'allow_always' | 'deny') is removed — replaced by shared ConfirmationResponse type
ConfirmationScope type ('tool_domain' | 'tool_all' | 'domain_all') is removed
MAX_SESSION_PERMISSIONS constant is removed
pushSessionPermission() function is removed

PendingConfirmation interface simplified: { resolve: (decision: 'allow' | 'deny') => void; reject: (error: Error) => void; tool: string; plugin: string; params: Record<string, unknown> } — no timerId, no domain, no tabId
New getToolPermission(state, pluginName, toolName): ToolPermission function — checks skipPermissions first, then pluginConfig.tools[tool] ?? pluginConfig.permission ?? 'off'
isToolEnabled() and isBrowserToolEnabled() functions are removed — replaced by getToolPermission()
ToolConfig type alias is removed
createState() uses new defaults: pluginPermissions: {}, no sessionPermissions, no permissions field

state.test.ts is rewritten for the new shape — tests getToolPermission resolution (skipPermissions override, per-tool override, plugin default, absent plugin = off, absent tool inherits plugin default)

userStories.title: Rewrite mcp-tool-dispatch.ts for the new permission model
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the browser tool dispatch to use getToolPermission instead of the old tier/domain system so that browser tools follow the same off/ask/auto model as plugin tools
acceptanceCriteria: handleBrowserToolCall checks getToolPermission(state, 'browser', toolName) instead of evaluatePermission()
Permission 'off': returns immediate error 'Tool X is currently disabled. Ask the user to enable it in the OpenTabs side panel.' with isError: true
Permission 'ask': sends confirmation request (no timeout), blocks until user responds, sends MCP progress notification if progressToken available
Permission 'auto': executes immediately with no checks
resolveToolDomain() function is removed — domain is no longer used for permission decisions
truncateParamsPreview() function is removed — full params are sent in confirmation request
isSessionAllowed() call is removed
No reference to CONFIRMATION_TIMEOUT_MS, evaluatePermission, ToolTier, or domain-based logic

When user responds 'allow' with alwaysAllow=true, the tool permission is updated to 'auto' via state.pluginPermissions mutation and persisted via savePluginPermissions, then callbacks.onToolConfigChanged() is called to notify MCP client of description change

mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts is rewritten with tests for: off→error, ask→deny, ask→allow (one-time), ask→allow with alwaysAllow (persists to auto), auto→immediate execution, skipPermissions=true overrides everything to auto
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts — Major rewrite of handleBrowserToolCall. Current flow (lines 164-263): isBrowserToolEnabled check → resolveToolDomain → isSessionAllowed → evaluatePermission → if ask: sendConfirmationRequest with timeout. New flow: getToolPermission(state, 'browser', toolBaseName) → switch on result: 'off' → return error, 'auto' → proceed to execution, 'ask' → sendConfirmationRequest (modified, no timeout) → handle response. Remove resolveToolDomain() (lines 57-94), truncateParamsPreview() (lines 100-116). The sendConfirmationRequest function in extension-protocol.ts also needs updating (see US-007).
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts — Full rewrite. Current tests (lines 315-602) test tier/domain/session permission flows. Replace with tests for the 3-state model.

For plugin tool dispatch (handlePluginToolCall): it currently has NO permission checks. Add a getToolPermission check at the top of handlePluginToolCall, before schema validation. Same 3-way switch: off→error, ask→block with confirmation, auto→proceed. This is a key part of the redesign — plugin tools now participate in the permission model.

The confirmation request sent to the extension should use the new simplified shape: { id, tool, plugin, params } — no domain, no tabId, no timeoutMs.

userStories.title: Rewrite extension-protocol.ts confirmation flow (no timeout, simplified payload)

acceptanceCriteria: sendConfirmationRequest() no longer sets a setTimeout — the Promise hangs indefinitely until resolved by user response or WebSocket disconnect
sendConfirmationRequest() sends the new ConfirmationRequest shape: { id, tool, plugin, params } — no domain, tabId, paramsPreview, or timeoutMs
sendConfirmationRequest() stores simplified PendingConfirmation (no timerId)
rejectAllPendingConfirmations() still works for WebSocket disconnect cleanup — rejects all pending promises
The 'confirmation.response' handler resolves with the decision ('allow' or 'deny') and passes alwaysAllow flag back to the caller
extension-protocol.test.ts confirmation tests are updated for the new flow

npm run lint passes

Change the wire payload from { id, tool, domain, tabId, paramsPreview, timeoutMs } to { id, tool, plugin, params }. The function should return Promise<{ action: 'allow' | 'deny'; alwaysAllow: boolean }> instead of resolving with just a ConfirmationDecision string. Remove CONFIRMATION_TIMEOUT_MS import.

userStories.title: Rewrite extension-handlers.ts for new permission config and confirmation handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want extension-handlers.ts to use the new plugin permission config and simplified confirmation response so that the server-extension protocol matches the new model
acceptanceCriteria: serializePluginForExtension() removes trustTier from payload, adds 'permission' field (resolved plugin-level permission), and each tool has 'permission' instead of 'enabled'
handleConfirmationResponse() validates the new response shape: { id, decision: 'allow'|'deny', alwaysAllow?: boolean } — no scope validation
handleConfirmationResponse() resolves PendingConfirmation with { action: decision, alwaysAllow } — no SessionPermissionRule creation
All session permission logic removed: no pushSessionPermission calls, no SessionPermissionRule imports
McpCallbacks removes onBrowserToolPolicyPersist — merged into single onPluginPermissionsPersist
config.setToolEnabled replaced by config.setToolPermission handler: { plugin, tool, permission } — validates permission is 'off'|'ask'|'auto', updates state.pluginPermissions, persists, notifies
config.setAllToolsEnabled replaced by config.setPluginPermission handler: { plugin, permission } — sets plugin-level default
config.setBrowserToolEnabled and config.setAllBrowserToolsEnabled removed — browser tools use the same handlers with plugin='browser'
config.setToolsEnabled removed (not needed)
VALID_CONFIRMATION_SCOPES removed
extension-handlers.test.ts rewritten for new handlers and confirmation flow
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts — Major rewrite. Key changes:
  1. serializePluginForExtension() (lines 113-156): remove trustTier field, change tool 'enabled: isToolEnabled(...)' to 'permission: getToolPermission(state, plugin.name, tool.name)', add plugin-level 'permission: getToolPermission(state, plugin.name, "")' — wait, that's not quite right. The plugin-level permission should come from state.pluginPermissions[plugin.name]?.permission ?? 'off'. Use getToolPermission for individual tools.
  2. handleConfirmationResponse() (lines 675-730): simplify — validate id exists in pendingConfirmations, validate decision is 'allow'|'deny', resolve the pending promise with { action: decision, alwaysAllow: params.alwaysAllow ?? false }. Remove all scope/SessionPermissionRule logic.
  3. Replace tool toggle handlers (lines 338-570): Remove handleConfigSetToolEnabled, handleConfigSetAllToolsEnabled, handleConfigSetToolsEnabled, handleConfigSetBrowserToolEnabled, handleConfigSetAllBrowserToolsEnabled. Add two new handlers: handleConfigSetToolPermission({ plugin, tool, permission }) and handleConfigSetPluginPermission({ plugin, permission }). Both validate params, mutate state.pluginPermissions, call callbacks.onPluginPermissionsPersist() and callbacks.onToolConfigChanged(), send plugins.changed notification.

- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.test.ts — Full rewrite of handler tests.

Also update the routing in extension-protocol.ts (lines 418-440) to route the new method names:
- 'config.setToolPermission' → handleConfigSetToolPermission
- 'config.setPluginPermission' → handleConfigSetPluginPermission
- Remove routes for old methods

userStories.title: Replace ConfirmationDialog with retro Dialog modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tool approval dialog to be a proper modal dialog with no countdown timer so that I can take my time to review and approve tool calls
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmationDialog component uses the retro Dialog primitive (Radix modal with overlay) instead of an inline card
Dialog has yellow bg-primary header with 'Approve Tool' title

Dialog body shows: tool name (font-mono), plugin name (font-sans), collapsible parameters section (collapsed by default using HTML details/summary), and 'Always allow this tool' checkbox with 'Sets permission to Auto' helper text
Dialog footer has 'Deny' button (variant=outline) and 'Allow' button (primary yellow)
No countdown timer, no CountdownBar component, no Progress bar
No AllowAlwaysButton dropdown with scope options
X button in header acts as Deny
Overlay click does NOT dismiss the dialog (preventClose behavior)
Multiple pending confirmations show '1 of N' counter in header with prev/next in footer
When Allow clicked with checkbox checked: sends { decision: 'allow', alwaysAllow: true }
When Allow clicked without checkbox: sends { decision: 'allow' }
When Deny clicked: sends { decision: 'deny' }
ConfirmationDialog.stories.tsx updated with new component stories
ConfirmationDialog.test.ts updated
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx — Full rewrite. Replace the inline card (lines 94-183) with a retro Dialog modal. Import { Dialog } from './retro/Dialog'. Import { Button } from './retro/Button'. The new component structure: ```tsx 0}> e.preventDefault()} onEscapeKeyDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}> Approve Tool {count > 1 && {idx+1} of {count}}
Tool
{current.tool}
Plugin
{current.plugin}
Parameters
{JSON.stringify(current.params, null, 2)}
Sets permission to Auto {count > 1 && prev/next buttons} ``` Update ConfirmationData type: remove domain, timeoutMs, receivedAt, paramsPreview. Add plugin: string, params: Record. Remove CountdownBar component and AllowAlwaysButton component entirely. For the X button behavior: the retro Dialog.Header includes a built-in close X button that triggers DialogPrimitive.Close. Wrap the dialog content so that closing = deny. Use onOpenChange to call deny when the dialog is closed. For Radix Dialog: use onInteractOutside={e => e.preventDefault()} to prevent overlay dismiss, and onEscapeKeyDown similarly. The X button should trigger onRespond with 'deny'. Stories: update ConfirmationDialog.stories.tsx to show the new modal dialog. Also update useServerNotifications.ts to remove timeout auto-removal logic and adapt to new ConfirmationData shape.

userStories.title: Replace ToolRow Switch with 3-state permission selector
userStories.description: As a user, I want each tool row to show a 3-state selector (Off/Ask/Auto) instead of an on/off switch so that I can control tool permissions granularly
acceptanceCriteria: ToolRow component renders a 3-state dropdown/select control instead of a Switch
The control shows the current permission state: Off, Ask, or Auto
Changing the value calls onPermissionChange(toolName, newPermission) instead of onToggle()
The control uses retro styling: border-2 border-border font-mono text-xs, matching the neo-brutalist theme
When skipPermissions is active, the control is disabled with reduced opacity
ToolRow.stories.tsx updated with all three states

npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx — Replace the Switch (line 39-44) with a native <select> element styled with retro classes. Three <option> values: 'off', 'ask', 'auto'. Display text: 'Off', 'Ask', 'Auto'. Style: border-2 border-border rounded bg-card font-mono text-xs px-1 py-0.5. Change the prop from onToggle: () => void to onPermissionChange: (tool: string, permission: ToolPermission) => void. Keep the enabled prop for ToolIcon coloring — derive it from permission !== 'off'.

The select should be small and compact since it sits in a constrained row. Use a native <select> for simplicity — no need for Radix Select here. Style it to match the retro theme: square corners (rounded resolves to 0), solid border, flat shadow on focus.

Add disabled prop for skipPermissions mode.

Update ToolRow.stories.tsx and ToolIcon.tsx if needed (ToolIcon uses enabled prop for coloring — change to check permission !== 'off').

userStories.title: Update E2E test fixtures and permissions E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the E2E tests updated so that they test the new 3-state permission model end-to-end
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/fixtures.ts OpentabsConfig interface uses the new 'plugins' shape
e2e/fixtures.ts default test config uses skipPermissions or appropriate plugin permissions for test tools

e2e/permissions.e2e.ts is fully rewritten to test the new model: off→error response, ask→dialog appears→allow, ask→dialog→deny, ask→allow with alwaysAllow (permission persists), auto→immediate execution, skipPermissions bypasses all
Confirmation dialog E2E tests verify: no countdown timer, proper retro Dialog modal, Always Allow checkbox behavior, X button = deny
All other E2E test files that reference trustTier, toolConfig, browserToolPolicy are updated

hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts updated for new toolConfig → pluginPermissions
plugin-management.e2e.ts removes isOfficial checks

userStories.notes:
Files to modify:
- e2e/fixtures.ts — Update OpentabsConfig interface (lines 185-190) to use new shape. Update the default fixture config (line 506 and surrounding) — currently sets OPENTABS_SKIP_PERMISSIONS: '1'. For the permission-specific E2E tests, we need a fixture that does NOT skip permissions.
- e2e/permissions.e2e.ts — Full rewrite. Current tests (lines 5-552) test the old tier system: observe auto-allow, interact confirmation+timeout, deny returns PERMISSION_DENIED, allow-always with scope, session persistence, trusted domains, sensitive tiers, browserToolPolicy. Replace with tests for:
  1. Tool with permission 'off': agent calls it, gets error message about disabled
  2. Tool with permission 'ask': agent calls it, side panel shows Dialog modal (not card), user clicks Allow, tool executes
  3. Tool with permission 'ask': user clicks Deny, agent gets error
  4. Tool with permission 'ask' + Always Allow checkbox: permission changes to 'auto', subsequent call executes without dialog
  5. Tool with permission 'auto': executes immediately
  6. skipPermissions=true: all tools execute immediately regardless of config
  7. Plugin-level permission: setting plugin to 'auto' makes all its tools auto
  8. Per-tool override: tool-level permission overrides plugin default
- e2e/side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts — Update for new permission selectors (was testing Switch toggle, now test 3-state selector).
- e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts — Update toolConfig references.
- e2e/plugin-management.e2e.ts — Remove isOfficial checks.

E2E tests need to interact with the new UI: <select> elements instead of Switch toggles, Dialog modal instead of inline card, etc. Use Playwright selectors for the new elements.
